message:
chore(beads): file ft-8pcyz envelope-conformance gap for 6 wa.* tools

Pure insertion in .beads/issues.jsonl (1 line added, 0 removed).

New bead: `ft-8pcyz — [HIGH] 6 wa.* tools (accounts, accounts_refresh,
cass_search, cass_status, cass_view, workflow_run) lack envelope
conformance`. Notes that these six MCP tools do not have the
FT-MCP-0001 envelope coverage the search/get_text/send/wait_for
suite now provides via `mcp_conformance.rs` / `mcp_conformance_
core_tools.rs` / `mcp_conformance_rules_test.rs`. Result: a drift
in the error envelope shape, hint wording, or TOON/JSON parity on
any of these six tools would ship silently. Filed HIGH because the
gap spans both the public-facing `workflow_run` (which is a write
surface) and `cass_*` (which reads operator-visible session
search state).

Filed so the six tools pick up matching conformance tests in the
next pass.

files.patch:

 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Zero ansi_byte_count after SIMD scan when flag is false; scan still runs to keep state.scan_state honest across chunks. Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.698143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.697874Z","close_reason":"Landed in commit 49937d56: storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing test pins the full StorageHandle reopen path. Asserts (1) fresh open with defer_fts_triggers=false installs all 3 output_segments_a[iud] triggers and synchronously indexes the first write, (2) reopen with defer_fts_triggers=false keeps all 3 triggers and synchronously indexes the second write via the trigger path. Combined with existing fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle (SQL-level true→false phase test) and fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip (5-iteration stress), the ft-ih4tm bidirectional contract is now pinned at both the connection and StorageHandle layers. Added Debug+Clone on StorageConfig for test ergonomics. All 3 tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-5qsbp","title":"[storage] Existing v0 DB init marks unrepaired old tables current","description":"Fresh-eyes review of the recent storage migration idempotency refactor found that initialize_schema's current==0 && !needs_init branch runs SCHEMA_SQL and immediately sets user_version to SCHEMA_VERSION. SCHEMA_SQL uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, so any old table that already exists is not altered. The branch only special-cases audit_actions.correlation_id, leaving other current columns/tables on existing old tables unrepaired while marking the DB current. Root cause: v0 partial DB handling treats CREATE IF NOT EXISTS as a schema repair mechanism. Fix by running the migration plan after SCHEMA_SQL so per-version idempotent helpers repair existing tables before user_version is marked current; add regression assertions to migration_runner_simulated_upgrade.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:41:40.693495Z","created_by":"RoseCat","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:11:02.853236Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","migration","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3636,"issue_id":"ft-5qsbp","author":"RoseCat","text":"Reopened: Fresh-eyes follow-up found a sparse-v0 miss in the previous repair: pre-repair helpers that create indexes must only run when their base table already exists.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:11:02Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-5qsbp","title":"[storage] Existing v0 DB init marks unrepaired old tables current","description":"Fresh-eyes review of the recent storage migration idempotency refactor found that initialize_schema's current==0 && !needs_init branch runs SCHEMA_SQL and immediately sets user_version to SCHEMA_VERSION. SCHEMA_SQL uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, so any old table that already exists is not altered. The branch only special-cases audit_actions.correlation_id, leaving other current columns/tables on existing old tables unrepaired while marking the DB current. Root cause: v0 partial DB handling treats CREATE IF NOT EXISTS as a schema repair mechanism. Fix by running the migration plan after SCHEMA_SQL so per-version idempotent helpers repair existing tables before user_version is marked current; add regression assertions to migration_runner_simulated_upgrade.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:41:40.693495Z","created_by":"RoseCat","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:23.826902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:23.826438Z","close_reason":"Fresh-eyes follow-up fixed sparse v0 DB handling: pre-SCHEMA_SQL repairs now run only when audit_actions/workflow_step_logs/events already exist, so missing tables are created by the normal schema/migration path. Added migration_runner_sparse_v0_database_initializes. Verified rustfmt --edition 2024, git diff --check, remote rch cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib migration_runner_ (2 passed), and UBS changed-file scan with only pre-existing storage.rs inventory remaining.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","migration","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3636,"issue_id":"ft-5qsbp","author":"RoseCat","text":"Reopened: Fresh-eyes follow-up found a sparse-v0 miss in the previous repair: pre-repair helpers that create indexes must only run when their base table already exists.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461737Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461447Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 691661d2 (Option a: round to 2 decimals, matching pane 5 style precedent). canonicalize() in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs now matches 'lexical_weight' | 'semantic_weight' if the child is a number, computes (f*100.0).round()/100.0, and stores it via serde_json::Number::from_f64 → Value::Number. The golden value is exactly 0.3 / 0.7 after round-trip because ryu emits shortest round-trip form for f64 0.3 / 0.7. wa_search.json golden pinned to the canonical 0.3 / 0.7 values. Kept the 30/70 split readable in the golden (reviewer can still see intent at a glance) instead of zeroing like score/semantic_score. Semantic assertions in assert_search_success_data at :446 already skip these fields so no test-logic change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-8ku8p","title":"gemini.model.used lacks near-miss golden for broad model anchors","description":"Rule: `gemini.model.used` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:2050` matches broad prompt text such as `Using model`, `Model:`, and `Switched to model`, then extracts `gemini-*` via regex.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/` is positive-only:
- `model_used.txt`
- `model_used.expect.json`
- `model_used_alt.txt`
- `model_used_alt.expect.json`

There is no committed near-miss `.txt` + `.expect.json` pair proving that generic docs/config/help text containing `Model:` or `Using model` does not spuriously fire `gemini.model.used`. Add a negative golden to freeze that false-positive boundary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:17.118958Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:04.627388Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:04.627016Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current checkout: gemini.model.used near-miss corpus files and fixture wiring are present, and git history shows commit 2bd522e3 landed this bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3614,"issue_id":"ft-8ku8p","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current checkout already contains the requested negative golden coverage. The Gemini corpus directory includes model_used_near_miss.txt and model_used_near_miss.expect.json, whose content is generic help/config text and an empty expected detection list. The builtin fixture table also already includes gemini_model_used_negative at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/patterns/builtin.json lines 341-344. git log for these paths shows commit 2bd522e3 with subject: test(patterns): gemini.model.used anchor coverage + near-miss goldens (ft-8ku8p). No additional corpus or wiring change is needed in this checkout.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-8l0ud","title":"[storage] Migration helpers return StorageError instead of crate Error","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib stream_channel fails before running tests because storage migration helper functions return Result<(), StorageError> from final map_err expressions where the crate Result alias expects Result<(), Error>. Fix by coercing StorageError through ?/Ok at the boundary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:08:02.253133Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.306113Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.305850Z","close_reason":"Fixed storage migration helper Result boundary by applying ? to StorageError map_err results and returning Ok(()). Verified the compile blocker cleared under the focused rch cargo test filters stream_channel, overflow, and drop_burst.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","correctness","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-8mgti","title":"escape parser emits wrong kitty transmit-and-display action code","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:05:01.954836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:08.245193Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:08.244869Z","close_reason":"landed in 98f5360a: KittyImage::TransmitDataAndDisplay now formats with action T to match parse_apc, plus a display->parse regression test in apc.rs; verified locally with scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib kitty_transmit_and_display_display_roundtrips_via_parse_apc -- --nocapture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-8pcyz","title":"[HIGH] 6 wa.* tools (accounts, accounts_refresh, cass_search, cass_status, cass_view, workflow_run) lack envelope conformance","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:22:09.838158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:22:31.361219Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","mcp","review"],"comments":[{"id":3637,"issue_id":"ft-8pcyz","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

Manifest fixture `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`
advertises 29 `wa.*` tools. Per-tool envelope conformance coverage now exists
for 23 of them across:

* mcp_conformance.rs (wa.rules_list)
* mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (wa.search, wa.get_text, wa.send, wa.wait_for)
* mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs (wa.mission_*, wa.tx_*)
* mcp_conformance_rules_test.rs (wa.rules_test; added this session)
* Per-tool goldens in tests/golden_robot_envelope/ (wa.state, wa.reserve/release/reservations, wa.events*, agent_inventory)

The following 6 tools have ZERO call-and-assert-envelope coverage:

* `wa.accounts` — account listing
* `wa.accounts_refresh` — account rotation trigger (mutating)
* `wa.cass_search` — coding-agent session search
* `wa.cass_status` — cass daemon status
* `wa.cass_view` — cass session detail
* `wa.workflow_run` — workflow execution (mutating, dry-run + live)

Verification:
  for t in accounts accounts_refresh cass_search cass_status cass_view workflow_run; do
    grep -rn "\"wa\\.$t\"" crates/frankenterm-core/tests/*conformance*.rs
  done
returns zero hits for all six.

# Why this matters

Same reasoning as ft-4o5mb: `conformance_mcp_manifest` freezes the MANIFEST
(tool schemas) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope
round-trip. Without per-tool tests, a handler refactor can pass manifest
conformance while silently emitting a non-FT-MCP-0001-shaped response body
or a drifted `data` schema.

`wa.accounts_refresh` and `wa.workflow_run` are also MUTATING / audit-logged,
so drift there is a security-relevant regression surface, not just a schema
one.

# Shape of fix

Mirror the per-tool golden pattern in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs / 
mcp_conformance_rules_test.rs:

1. spawn_client() → call_tool(name, …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape()
3. assert_matches_golden() for the canonical shape
4. Pin invalid-format (yaml) + missing-required-arg rejection envelopes

For cass_* tools, the external `cass` binary needs either stubbing or a
feature-gated live test (see how fake-wezterm is handled at
mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:59 for the stubbing pattern).

# Scope guard

One new file per family:
* mcp_conformance_accounts.rs (2 tools)
* mcp_conformance_cass.rs (3 tools, needs cass stub)
* mcp_conformance_workflow_run.rs (1 tool, needs workflow registry)

~600 LOC total. No production changes.

# Out of scope

Fixing any drift discovered by the new tests (separate beads per tool).","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:22:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-8qd0l","title":"sync_fts_for_pane skips seq=0 segment on fresh pane (off-by-one in WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq)","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15884 sync_fts_for_pane`:

```rust
let progress = get_fts_pane_progress_sync(conn, pane_id)?;
let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);
```

When no progress exists (fresh pane, deferred-indexing path), `last_seq` defaults to 0. Then `get_unindexed_segments_sync` queries with `WHERE seq > ?2` (exclusive) — so a pane's seq=0 segment is NEVER selected by the first catchup pass.

`append_segment_sync` (storage.rs:12317) computes next_seq via `COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0)` so every pane's FIRST segment is seq=0. The sync-trigger path indexes it correctly (trigger fires on every INSERT). The deferred-catchup path does not.

# Discovery

Found while landing ft-wk5fo (opt-in deferred FTS triggers, commit d74401c6). The initial ft-wk5fo regression tests used seq 0..500 and failed with `99 expected 100` because the first round missed seq=0. Tests were adjusted to start at seq=1 to ship the deferred-mode path, but the underlying bug remained. This bead tracks fixing the bug proper.

# Fix options

(a) Discriminate "no progress" from "indexed up to 0" via Option in the WHERE clause:
    - When `progress == None`: query `WHERE seq >= 0` (or just omit the lower bound).
    - When `progress == Some(p)`: query `WHERE seq > p.last_indexed_seq`.
    Requires refactoring get_unindexed_segments_sync to take an `Option<u64>` bound.

(b) Add a sentinel column to fts_pane_progress like `initialized: bool` and check it in sync_fts_for_pane.

(c) Seed fts_pane_progress with `last_indexed_seq = -1` when creating the pane entry. Requires i64 column or underflow trick.

(a) is cleanest.

# Regression test

Add `fts_deferred_mode_indexes_pane_seq_zero`: fresh pane, one segment at seq=0 via append_segment_sync-style path, DROP TRIGGERS, run sync_fts_on_startup, assert the seg is in FTS.

# Severity

MEDIUM — only bites the deferred-indexing path (currently opt-in, default off). Trigger path is unaffected. But every fresh pane's FIRST segment is silently skipped by the catchup engine, which is latent until deferred mode goes default-on.

# Related

- ft-wk5fo (parent): introduced the deferred path that exposes this bug. Closed with seq-starts-at-1 workaround in the regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:21.050180Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.883029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.882777Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8uj10","title":"event identity key should ignore extracted object insertion order","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:19.767134Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:49.591423Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:49.590786Z","close_reason":"Already landed in 765743d5 (fix(events): sort extracted object keys before event identity hash + ft-gc4hz circuit-breaker test).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.893772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
@@ -168,7 +169,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-d70a9","title":"claude_code.auth.login_required lacks alternate anchor golden fixtures","description":"Rule: `claude_code.auth.login_required` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:1705` ships four auth-flow anchors: `To sign in`, `login required`, `please authenticate`, and `auth required`.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/claude_code/` only has one positive golden pair for the first phrasing:
- `login_required.txt`
- `login_required.expect.json`

There is a near-miss pair, but no committed positive fixture for the other shipped browser-auth phrasings. Add at least one alternate positive `.txt` + `.expect.json` golden exercising a non-`To sign in` variant so pack drift on login prompts is caught before the rule silently narrows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:16.439731Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:22:20.553440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:22:20.553124Z","close_reason":"Added 3 alternate-anchor golden fixtures for claude_code.auth.login_required covering the remaining 3 of 4 shipped anchors beyond 'To sign in': login_required_v2 ('login required'), login_required_authenticate ('please authenticate'), login_required_auth_required ('auth required'). Each .txt contains a literal anchor from patterns.rs:1709-1714; each .expect.json mirrors the existing login_required.expect.json schema exactly with matched_text swapped for the covered anchor. Pack drift on any browser-auth phrasing now trips corpus_fixtures_match_expected.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dijpe","title":"connector_inbound_bridge: dedup uses attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms as 'now', lets replay bypass the window","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs:495` in `route_signal`:

```rust
if let Some(ref cid) = signal.correlation_id {
    let now_ms = signal.timestamp_ms;
    if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) {
        ...
    }
}
```

The bridge uses `signal.timestamp_ms` — explicitly documented at line 132 as 'Timestamp of the signal AT THE SOURCE' — as the 'now_ms' argument that drives dedup eviction. Every connector (github, slack, webhook, etc.) sends this field and the bridge trusts it as its clock.

## Concrete replay bypass

`SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record` calls `evict_expired(now_ms)` which pops entries where `now_ms - inserted > ttl_ms`. An attacker-controlled upstream can:

1. Send signal X with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=0` → inserted_at=0 in cache.
2. Send signal X again with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1` → evict_expired sees `now=ttl+1`, front-entry at inserted=0, `ttl+1 > ttl` → pops 'spam' from cache. Then check finds cache empty → inserts a new 'spam' entry → returns TRUE (treated as new). Dedup bypassed.

Repeat indefinitely by claiming monotonically-increasing timestamps. Every signal is seen as 'new' and routed to the pattern engine + EventBus, duplicating every downstream side effect (workflow triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Scope

Only bites the inbound bridge, whose callers ARE the external connectors (by design — this is the agent-owned ingest surface for webhook/API signals). A buggy connector that sends stale `timestamp_ms` values can unknowingly bypass dedup too; it doesn't require malice.

Outbound bridge doesn't have this shape — it uses its own `now_ms` from `SystemTime::now()` for all internal state.

## Fix

Drive dedup bookkeeping from the LOCAL system clock, not the signal's claimed timestamp. The signal.timestamp_ms field stays useful as analytics/telemetry data (end-to-end source→sink latency, etc.) but must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

Change `route_signal` to:

```rust
let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .unwrap_or_default()
    .as_millis() as u64;
if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) { ... }
```

## Regression test

- `dedup_rejects_replay_with_future_timestamp`: route X with cid='r' and timestamp=0, then route X with cid='r' and timestamp=ttl+1. Assert the second is still flagged `deduplicated=true` under a fresh bridge with a short ttl. Before the fix, the second call returns `deduplicated=false` because evict_expired sees now=ttl+1 and pops 'r'.

## Severity

MEDIUM — requires an external caller to either claim stale/future timestamps or replay signals. Doesn't crash. But every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR comment) can be re-fired without bound by a single misbehaving connector.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:45:15.242201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.688022Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.687664Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0716e112. Replaced signal.timestamp_ms → SystemTime::now() in route_signal's dedup clock argument so an upstream claiming future timestamps can no longer evict live dedup entries and bypass the window. Regression test bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp (timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 on replay → still flagged deduplicated=true) covers the attacker-replay shape. Verified: 44 connector_inbound_bridge:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-djpi3","title":"[mcp] wa.events_annotate returns placeholder changed=null instead of real mutation bit","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4310-4369, wa.events_annotate always returns McpEventMutationData { changed: None, ... } even though sibling mutation tools wa.events_triage and wa.events_label return a real changed bool when the mutation is a noop vs a real write. This makes a success envelope indistinguishable between setting a new note, re-sending the same note, and clearing a note that was already absent.\n\nImpact: MCP callers cannot tell whether wa.events_annotate actually mutated state, so success envelopes carry placeholder data instead of the observable result.\n\nFix direction: derive a stable changed bit in the tool path (or storage helper) by comparing pre/post note state, and add a regression test that covers both changed=true and changed=false cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:17.651562Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:43:17.733444Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:43:17.733262Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0a865e6f: wa.events_annotate now derives changed bit from diff, not placeholder","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-dqsev","title":"[bug] delete_session runs 3 DELETEs without a transaction — partial failure yields orphaned checkpoints","description":"# Bug

\`session_restore::delete_session\` at \`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:1003\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

Three independent DELETEs, each in its own implicit rusqlite
transaction. If step 1 succeeds but step 2 fails (lock contention,
disk full, IO error, cancellation), we leave the database in a
tornn-inconsistent state:

  - Option A: pane_states DELETED, checkpoints still present,
    session still present → \`load_checkpoint_by_id\` returns
    Some(checkpoint) but with empty \`pane_states\` → restore
    produces an empty-pane session that looks clean but carries no
    terminal state.

  - Option B: pane_states + checkpoints DELETED, session row
    still present → \`list_sessions\` shows the session, but
    \`load_latest_checkpoint\` returns None. Next \`delete_session\`
    call's third DELETE succeeds → session is eventually cleaned
    up, but the window is observable.

The other deletion-style paths in session_restore.rs DO wrap their
mutations in \`conn.transaction()\` (see lines 626, 673). delete_session
is the anomaly.

# Fix

Wrap the three DELETEs in a single \`conn.transaction()\` that
commits on success or rolls back on any intermediate error.

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let mut conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    let tx = conn.transaction()?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    tx.commit()?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

# Test

Regression test: inject mid-operation failure (e.g., by DELETING a
session that references a checkpoint whose pane_state rows have a
trigger or hook that fails). Without a transaction, the partial
state is observable; with the fix, the session row remains.

For a simpler test, assert that after a successful delete_session
there are zero rows in all three tables for the target session_id,
AND that commit/rollback atomicity holds across a wrapping PRAGMA.

# Scope

Single function, ~6 LOC change. No schema migration. Preserves the
return value contract (true if session row was deleted).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:06.907980Z","created_by":"pane3-delete-tx","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944608Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1a090629. Wrapped delete_session's three DELETE statements in a single conn.transaction() with commit on success. Pattern lifted from existing transactional helpers at session_restore.rs:626 + :673. Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl pre-existing breakage — change is mechanical and preserves the return-value contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomicity","session","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910506Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 26841c83: build.rs now uses .ok().filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty()) so SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='' and whitespace-only values collapse to None (fallback to wall-clock). Some('0') preserved as a valid epoch. Code comment documents the rationale. Build.rs has no cargo-test-reachable helpers, so no unit test added; verified via standalone rustc --edition 2024 parse + runtime dry-run on ['', '   ', '0', '1700000000', None].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-e018k","title":"event_identity_key nested Object insertion order leaks into hash (recursive fix for 765743d5)","description":"765743d5 sorted top-level keys of extracted object, but nested Object/Array values went through serde_json::to_string which preserves IndexMap insertion order. Two logically-equivalent events with nested {'context': {'a':1,'b':2}} vs {'context': {'b':2,'a':1}} hashed differently → dedup-to-two. Fix landed in 50ff1eb0: canonicalize_json_value recursively sorts all nested Object keys before serialization (arrays stay ordered per JSON spec, but their contained Objects get canonicalized). Regression tests event_identity_key_ignores_nested_extracted_object_insertion_order + event_identity_key_canonicalizes_objects_inside_arrays.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.936724Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227773Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227512Z","close_reason":"Landed in 50ff1eb0 (fix(events): recursively canonicalize nested Object keys in normalized_extracted)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
